item.title: "Final, agreed upon text" of U.S.-Iran peace deal reached, Pakistan says
item.description: Iran's foreign minister said a deal has "never been closer" and that the details of a memorandum of understanding with the U.S. will be shared publicly "in due course."

item.title: New Yorker confronts unruly Knicks fans: "This is our city"
item.description: When violence erupted near a New York Knicks watch party on Monday, one New Yorker stood up to the unruly crowd.

item.title: Knicks fan who intervened when crowd got destructive says he'd do it again: "I did it for my city"
item.description: On Monday night, after the Knicks lost to the Spurs in Game 3 of the NBA Finals, fans took to the streets, and it turned chaotic. But one father stood up when he saw people being destructive. CBS News' Jared Ochacher has the story.

item.title: DOJ paves way for Paramount Skydance to buy Warner Bros. Discovery
item.description: Paramount Skydance's $110 billion acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery would not harm U.S. consumers or weaken competition, DOJ antitrust enforcers said.

item.title: DOJ asks appeals court to let Trump's name stay on Kennedy Center
item.description: A federal judge denied a last-minute attempt by the Trump administration to stop the removal of President Trump's name from the Kennedy Center.

item.title: USMNT to face Paraguay in World Cup opening game in LA
item.description: The U.S. Men's national team will kick off its World Cup campaign on Friday night. They are set to take on Paraguay on home turf in Los Angeles. CBS News correspondent Nicole Valdes reports.

item.title: Los Angeles FC defender Aaron Long says a U.S. win against Paraguay would "settle all the nerves"
item.description: After a four-year-long wait, the 26 men chosen to represent the U.S. will take the pitch against Paraguay on Friday night in their first match of the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Aaron Long, defender for Los Angeles FC and member of the 2022 U.S. Men's National Team in Qatar, joins with analysis.

item.title: Is the U.S. on track to an agreement with Iran?
item.description: A U.S. official said they are 80-85% confident that a deal with Iran will be signed in the coming days, but it's not 100% certain. Washington and Tehran also shared different terms on a potential deal. CBS News political reporter Zak Hudak has more.

item.title: Human remains found in 2 Twin Cities lakes 33 years ago identified
item.description: Investigators in Washington County, Minnesota, say they've identified the human remains found in two Twin Cities lakes more than three decades ago.

item.title: Candidate confusion in Alaska Senate race as Republicans with same name compete for same seat
item.description: Two Republicans with the same name, incumbent Sen. Dan S. Sullivan and challenger Dan J. Sullivan, are battling on the ballot in Alaska. CBS News election law expert and contributor David Becker joins to unpack the campaign confusion.

item.title: Details on SpaceX debut and DOJ approving Paramount merger with Warner
item.description: SpaceX officially went public on Friday, making history as the largest initial public offering of all time and making Elon Musk a trillionaire. Also on Friday, the Justice Department gave the green light for a merger between CBS News' parent company, Paramount Skydance, and Warner Bros. Discovery. Jo Ling Kent has more.

item.title: White House hosting $60 million UFC fight on Trump's 80th birthday
item.description: A federal judge has ruled the White House can host UFC matches on its South Lawn on Sunday, which marks President Trump's 80th birthday. CBS News political director Fin Gómez, along with political strategists Chuck Rocha and Leslie Shedd, join with analysis.

item.title: Justice Department approves Paramount-Warner Bros. merger
item.description: The Justice Department has given the merger between CBS News' parent company, Paramount Skydance, and Warner Bros. Discovery the green light. CBS News MoneyWatch correspondent Kelly O'Grady has the latest.

item.title: U.S. deports Afghani and Iranian migrants to Central African Republic
item.description: A U.S. official tells CBS News that the Trump administration deported about 20 migrants from countries including Afghanistan and Iran on Friday, sending them to the Central African Republic. CBS News immigration correspondent Camilo Montoya-Galvez reports.

item.title: SpaceX stock closes up 19% on first day of trading
item.description: SpaceX launched onto Wall Street on Friday with the largest initial public offering in history. CBS News MoneyWatch correspondent Kelly O'Grady has the details.

item.title: FISA surveillance powers set to expire
item.description: The U.S.' foreign surveillance powers are set to expire Friday after the House rejected a short-term FISA extension, as some lawmakers opposed President Trump's interim pick for director of national intelligence, Bill Pulte. CBS News intelligence and national security reporter Olivia Gazis has the details.

item.title: Judge rules UFC fight can go on; Kennedy Center board tries to stop Trump's name removal
item.description: A federal judge has given the go-ahead for a UFC fight to go forward on the South Lawn of the White House on Sunday. At the same time, the Kennedy Center's board is pushing to keep President Trump's name on the building. CBS News' Katrina Kaufman has more.

item.title: White House and Iran hint at major progress in peace talks
item.description: Is a deal between the U.S. and Iran finally within reach? CBS News' Taurean Small and Elliot Ackerman have the latest.

item.title: How Elon Musk accumulated power in the SpaceX market debut
item.description: SpaceX officially became the world's largest IPO ever on Friday, but the way it got there took a less-than-conventional road. A Politico report drew parallels between Elon Musk's handling of his market debut and President Trump's executive orders. Yasmin Khorram, Politico tech and economic policy reporter, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: SpaceX stock soars on first day of trading following record-breaking IPO
item.description: SpaceX's stock closed the day at $160.95 after making its debut on the Nasdaq exchange.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: At least 1 killed, 9 hospitalized in Texas mass shooting; suspect dead
item.description: The suspect, Victor Mata Villarreal, was wanted for attempted murder of a police officer after shooting at law enforcement during a vehicle chase earlier this week, officials said.

item.title: Watch: Pentagon releases 6 new UFO videos
item.description: The Defense Department released a third batch of UFO files on Friday, three weeks after its second drop. These are all of the videos in the latest tranche, plus analysis from astrophysicist Avi Loeb.

item.title: Good Samaritan New Yorker speaks out after decrying vandalism following Knicks game
item.description: Balladoli Miese, a New Yorker from Puerto Rico, calmed a rowdy crowd and asked them to avoid damaging public property after a Knicks loss on Monday. "It's our city, we should not be breaking our city," Miese said. CBS News' Jared Ochacher reports.

item.title: U.S. military making plans to secure Iran's nuclear materials, sources say
item.description: Military planners have discussed contingencies that would involve U.S. forces helping secure Iran's nuclear materials if a deal is reached, according to U.S. officials familiar with knowledge of the ongoing planning.

item.title: Pentagon releases 3rd batch of UFO files: "Are you seeing this?"
item.description: The Pentagon on Friday released a new group of documents and videos related to UFOs, or UAPs, with 72 more documents, images and recordings.

item.title: A key spy tool is expiring due to inaction in Congress. Here's what that means.
item.description: Lawmakers have long sounded the alarm about the risks of letting Section 702 expire. But there's debate over what a lapse in the law actually means.

item.title: FDA authorizes emergency use of drug to fight screwworms in pets
item.description: Nitenpyram is the first generic animal drug authorized to treat screwworms in dogs and cats, according to federal regulators.

item.title: Iran-linked group claims hack of FBI drones, threatens World Cup, monitor says
item.description: An Iran​-linked hacker group claims to have breached FBI drones and has threatened to target the World Cup, a monitoring group says. The monitor disputes some of the other group's claims.

item.title: World Cup kicks off in Mexico with Shakira and a home team victory
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup opening ceremony festivities got underway a little over an hour before the first kickoff in Mexico City's Estadio Azteca.
